title: Goodyera fusca
taxonomy: Goodyera fusca is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Goodyera, which includes about 20 species of terrestrial orchids. Goodyera fusca is a type of lady's tresses orchid, characterized by its long, slender spikes of small, white flowers. The species is native to eastern North America, where it grows in shaded, woodland areas. Goodyera fusca is a relatively rare and endangered species in some parts of its range.

intro: Goodyera fusca, also known as the Rattlesnake Plantain, is a perennial orchid species native to North America, characterized by its unique, strap-like leaves and delicate white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which have been linked to several health benefits, including wound healing, fever reduction, and respiratory system support.
